% --- Table 1: Cohort Characteristics ---
\begin{table}[htbp]
\centering
\small
\caption{Baseline characteristics of the MIMIC-III analytic cohort within the 30-day observation window, stratified by outcome. \emph{Discharged} denotes patients censored alive before the window closes (the clinically informative censoring mechanism); \emph{Survived} denotes patients still alive at 30 days (administratively censored). Values are $n$ (\%) for categorical variables and mean $\pm$ SD or median [IQR] for continuous variables.}
\label{tab:cohort}
\begin{tabular}{l C{2.4cm} C{2.2cm} C{2.4cm} C{2.4cm}}
\toprule
 & \textbf{Overall} & \textbf{Died} & \textbf{Discharged} & \textbf{Survived} \\
 & (N=57,694) & (n=5,443) & (n=49,660) & (n=2,591) \\
\midrule
\multicolumn{5}{l}{\textit{Demographics}} \\[2pt]
\quad  Age, years (mean $\pm$ SD) & 55.1 $\pm$ 27.3 & 69.8 $\pm$ 17.2 & 54.2 $\pm$ 27.3 & 40.2 $\pm$ 31.1 \\
\quad Male sex & 32,248 (55.9\%) & 2,906 (53.4\%) & 27,869 (56.1\%) & 1,473 (56.9\%) \\
\quad Emergency admission & 41,580 (72.1\%) & 5,081 (93.3\%) & 34,969 (70.4\%) & 1,530 (59.1\%) \\
\quad Follow-up, days (median [IQR]) & 5.9 [3.5, 10.6] & 4.5 [1.6, 9.8] & 5.7 [3.6, 9.6] & 42.0 [35.0, 57.3] \\
\midrule
\multicolumn{5}{l}{\textit{Comorbidities (ICD-9)}} \\[2pt]
\quad Sepsis & 6,320 (11.0\%) & 1,777 (32.6\%) & 3,878 (7.8\%) & 665 (25.7\%) \\
\quad Heart failure & 13,434 (23.3\%) & 1,840 (33.8\%) & 11,036 (22.2\%) & 558 (21.5\%) \\
\quad Acute kidney injury & 11,359 (19.7\%) & 2,335 (42.9\%) & 8,176 (16.5\%) & 848 (32.7\%) \\
\quad Respiratory failure & 8,906 (15.4\%) & 2,460 (45.2\%) & 5,661 (11.4\%) & 785 (30.3\%) \\
\quad Pneumonia & 7,347 (12.7\%) & 1,332 (24.5\%) & 5,322 (10.7\%) & 693 (26.7\%) \\
\quad Diabetes & 13,900 (24.1\%) & 1,423 (26.1\%) & 12,078 (24.3\%) & 399 (15.4\%) \\
\quad Liver disease & 2,916 (5.1\%) & 538 (9.9\%) & 2,195 (4.4\%) & 183 (7.1\%) \\
\quad Malignancy & 6,730 (11.7\%) & 1,258 (23.1\%) & 5,184 (10.4\%) & 288 (11.1\%) \\
\quad COPD & 6,568 (11.4\%) & 870 (16.0\%) & 5,508 (11.1\%) & 190 (7.3\%) \\
\midrule
\multicolumn{5}{l}{\textit{Clinical Indicators}} \\[2pt]
\quad GCS impaired ($\leq 12$) & 30,190 (52.3\%) & 4,495 (82.6\%) & 24,358 (49.0\%) & 1,337 (51.6\%) \\
\quad Mechanical ventilation & 14,477 (25.1\%) & 3,492 (64.2\%) & 9,240 (18.6\%) & 1,745 (67.3\%) \\
\quad Vasopressor use & 6,836 (11.8\%) & 1,312 (24.1\%) & 5,134 (10.3\%) & 390 (15.1\%) \\
\quad Dialysis & 3,166 (5.5\%) & 596 (10.9\%) & 2,195 (4.4\%) & 375 (14.5\%) \\
\quad Positive blood culture & 5,448 (9.4\%) & 1,049 (19.3\%) & 3,546 (7.1\%) & 853 (32.9\%) \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

% --- Table 2: Data Structure ---
\begin{table}[htbp]
\centering
\caption{Summary of the discrete-time data structure within the 30-day observation window ($\Delta = 1$ days, $K = 30$ intervals). Clinically censored patients are those discharged alive before 30 days; administratively censored patients survived the full window.}
\label{tab:data-structure}
\begin{tabular}{l r}
\toprule
Unique admissions ($N$) & 57,694 \\
\midrule
\multicolumn{2}{l}{\textit{Outcome within observation window}} \\[2pt]
\quad Died within 30 days & 5,443 (9.4\%) \\
\quad Discharged alive $<$ 30 days & 49,660 (86.1\%) \\
\quad Survived $\geq$ 30 days & 2,591 (4.5\%) \\
\midrule
\multicolumn{2}{l}{\textit{Discrete-time structure}} \\[2pt]
\quad Discretization width ($\Delta$) & 1 days \\
\quad Maximum intervals ($K$) & 30 \\
\quad Observation window & 30 days \\
\quad Static covariates ($P$) & 17 \\
\quad Person-period rows ($\mathcal{R}$) & 509,944 \\
\quad Event rows ($y_{i,t}=1$) & 5,443 (1.067\% of $\mathcal{R}$) \\
\midrule
\multicolumn{2}{l}{\textit{Follow-up time, days}} \\[2pt]
\quad Overall: median [IQR] & 5.9 [3.5, 10.6] \\
\quad Decedents: median [IQR] & 4.5 [1.6, 9.8] \\
\quad Discharged: median [IQR] & 5.7 [3.6, 9.6] \\
\quad Survived $\geq$ 30 days & 2,591 patients \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

% --- Table 3: Covariate Descriptions ---
\begin{table}[htbp]
\centering
\small
\caption{Covariates in the design matrix $\mathbf{X}$ ($P = 17$). All variables except age are binary (0/1). ICD-9 codes shown in parentheses.}
\label{tab:covariates}
\begin{tabular}{r l l r}
\toprule
 & \textbf{Variable} & \textbf{Description} & \textbf{Prevalence} \\
\midrule
1 & \texttt{age\_std} & Age (standardized to $\mu=0$, $\sigma=1$) & 55.1 $\pm$ 27.3 yr \\
2 & \texttt{male} & Male sex & 55.9\% \\
3 & \texttt{emergency} & Emergency admission & 72.1\% \\
4 & \texttt{icd\_sepsis} & Sepsis (995.91/92, 038.x) & 11.0\% \\
5 & \texttt{icd\_hf} & Heart failure (428.x) & 23.3\% \\
6 & \texttt{icd\_resp} & Respiratory failure (518.8x) & 15.4\% \\
7 & \texttt{icd\_aki} & Acute kidney injury (584.x) & 19.7\% \\
8 & \texttt{gcs\_impaired} & GCS $\leq 12$ & 52.3\% \\
9 & \texttt{vasopressor} & Any vasopressor & 11.8\% \\
10 & \texttt{icd\_pneumonia} & Pneumonia (480--486) & 12.7\% \\
11 & \texttt{icd\_diabetes} & Diabetes (250.x) & 24.1\% \\
12 & \texttt{icd\_liver} & Liver disease (571.x) & 5.1\% \\
13 & \texttt{icd\_malignancy} & Malignancy (140--208) & 11.7\% \\
14 & \texttt{icd\_copd} & COPD (490--492, 494, 496) & 11.4\% \\
15 & \texttt{is\_ventilated} & Mechanical ventilation (proc 96.7x) & 25.1\% \\
16 & \texttt{received\_dialysis} & Hemodialysis (proc 39.95) & 5.5\% \\
17 & \texttt{positive\_blood\_cx} & Positive blood culture & 9.4\% \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

% --- Table 5: Beta Comparison (main text) ---
\begin{table}[t]
\centering
\small
\caption{Posterior mean covariate effects (log-odds ratio for daily hazard) under Model~A and Model~B. Difference column shows the attenuation correction from including frailty.}
\label{tab:beta-comparison}
\begin{tabular}{lrrr}
\toprule
\textbf{Covariate} & \textbf{Model A} & \textbf{Model B} & \textbf{Difference} \\

male & $-$0.092 & $-$0.119 & $-$0.027 \\
\rowcolor{rowgray} emergency & 0.902 & 1.074 & +0.172 \\
icd\_sepsis & 0.502 & 0.704 & +0.202 \\
\rowcolor{rowgray} icd\_hf & $-$0.099 & $-$0.131 & $-$0.031 \\
icd\_resp & 0.202 & 0.263 & +0.061 \\
\rowcolor{rowgray} icd\_aki & 0.193 & 0.245 & +0.051 \\
gcs\_impaired & 0.614 & 0.718 & +0.104 \\
\rowcolor{rowgray} vasopressor & $-$0.063 & $-$0.035 & +0.028 \\
icd\_pneumonia & $-$0.353 & $-$0.493 & $-$0.140 \\
\rowcolor{rowgray} icd\_diabetes & $-$0.139 & $-$0.188 & $-$0.049 \\
icd\_liver & 0.436 & 0.515 & +0.079 \\
\rowcolor{rowgray} icd\_malignancy & 0.661 & 0.852 & +0.190 \\
icd\_copd & $-$0.044 & $-$0.068 & $-$0.025 \\
\rowcolor{rowgray} is\_ventilated & 0.964 & 1.250 & +0.286 \\
received\_dialysis & 0.046 & 0.044 & $-$0.002 \\
\rowcolor{rowgray} positive\_blood\_cx & $-$0.286 & $-$0.406 & $-$0.120 \\
